stories.headline: Senate Begins Debate on SAVE America Act Amid Trump Veto Threats
stories.summary: The Senate opened debate on the SAVE America Act, aimed at overhauling U.S. election laws, after President Trump threatened to veto unrelated legislation until it passes. The push reflects midterm election strategy and Trump's demand for GOP unity on election integrity, though the bill currently lacks sufficient votes.

stories.headline: Trump Signs Executive Orders to Boost Housing Affordability
stories.summary: President Trump signed two executive orders reducing federal regulatory burdens on housing construction and streamlining mortgage processes for smaller banks. The actions respond to a nationwide shortage of over 4 million homes since the 2008 crisis, directing Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to issue $200 billion in mortgage bonds.

stories.headline: White House Creates Task Force to Eliminate Fraud
stories.summary: A new executive order establishes a Task Force targeting cybercrime, predatory schemes, and false 'Made in America' advertising claims. The initiative stems from midterm priorities to protect consumers and boost trust in domestic markets amid weakening economic signals.

stories.headline: Major Corporations Pledge Billions in U.S. Manufacturing Under 'Trump Effect'
stories.summary: Clarios ($6B), UCB ($5B), and Ford ($5B) announced massive domestic manufacturing investments credited to Trump administration policies favoring U.S. production. The White House is tracking these commitments as reshoring wins ahead of midterm elections, aiming to counter economic slowdowns.

stories.headline: House GOP Retreat Tackles Economy and Midterm Strategy
stories.summary: House Republicans held their annual policy retreat focused on a weakening economy, Middle East war funding, and Trump's legislative demands. Leaders discussed pairing military spending with Pentagon cuts via reconciliation alongside stalled housing and crypto bills.

stories.headline: Senate to Review Nuclear Energy Executive Orders Implementation
stories.summary: The Senate Energy & Natural Resources Committee will examine the Department of Energy's progress on Trump's May 2025 Nuclear Energy Executive Orders. The review reflects pushes for domestic energy independence amid global conflicts and rising AI datacenter power demands.

stories.headline: Congress Advances Balanced Budget Amendment
stories.summary: The House plans to consider a constitutional amendment requiring a federal balanced budget amid fiscal debates tied to war funding and economic weakness. GOP fiscal hawks and Trump's influence during retreats drive the push to enforce spending discipline before midterms.

stories.headline: Nvidia Unveils DLSS 5 with Generative AI for Real-Time Game Graphics
stories.summary: Nvidia announced DLSS 5 at GTC, using generative AI to deliver photo-realistic lighting and a projected 1,000,000x improvement in path tracing performance for RTX 50-series GPUs. The technology represents a shift from upscaling to AI-generated visual fidelity in real-time gaming.

stories.headline: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 Reasoning-Optimized Frontier Model
stories.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 on March 5, scoring 57.7% on SWE-Bench Pro and 92.8% on GPQA Diamond for advanced reasoning and coding tasks. The release came amid competitive pressures from Anthropic and controversy over OpenAI's Pentagon partnership.

stories.headline: Anthropic Blacklisted by Pentagon After Refusing Military AI Access
stories.summary: Anthropic faced a U.S. government blacklist after refusing Pentagon access to Claude for surveillance or autonomous weapons applications. The decision triggered consumer backlash with ChatGPT one-star reviews spiking 775% in solidarity, while OpenAI secured its own Pentagon deal.

stories.headline: Google DeepMind Launches Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite and Deep Think
stories.summary: Google unveiled Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite enabling 2.5x faster responses at $0.25 per million tokens, alongside Deep Think which solved open math problems on Erdős Conjectures. The releases reflect the industry shift toward smaller, denser models for cost-efficient inference.

stories.headline: Nvidia Announces New Inference Chips Using Groq Technology
stories.summary: Nvidia revealed new inference chips incorporating Groq technology alongside a multi-billion Google-Meta AI chip deal, fueling the infrastructure race. The moves address exploding compute demands for agentic AI and physical systems like humanoid robots.

stories.headline: Alibaba Launches Qwen 3.5 Small Model Series Outperforming Larger Rivals
stories.summary: Alibaba released the Qwen 3.5 series with models from 0.8B to 9B parameters that outperform larger competitors on benchmarks. The release reflects the growing trend toward intelligence density over raw scale, driven by enterprise cost pressures.

stories.headline: Samsung Ends Galaxy Z TriFold Sales After Just Three Months
stories.summary: Samsung discontinued its $2,899 Galaxy Z TriFold foldable phone only three months after launch due to disappointing sales. The device represented Samsung's push into tri-fold form factors but failed to gain consumer traction at its premium price point.

stories.headline: Wayve Secures Record $1.2 Billion Series D for Autonomous Driving
stories.summary: UK-based self-driving startup Wayve raised $1.2B backed by Mercedes and Stellantis, marking Europe's largest autonomous driving funding round. The deal signals heightened VC confidence in collaborations between AI startups and legacy automakers amid global competition in EVs and self-driving tech.

stories.headline: Replit Raises $400M Series D for AI Developer Tools
stories.summary: Cloud coding platform Replit secured $400M led by Georgian with participation from Accenture Ventures. Investor enthusiasm stems from Replit's AI-assisted collaborative environments as AI transforms software development workflows.

stories.headline: General Catalyst Seeks $10 Billion New Fund for AI Bets
stories.summary: General Catalyst is in talks to raise $10B following Thrive's $10B close and amid a wave of mega-funds from top VCs like Founders Fund ($6B). The record fundraising reflects AI hype-driven dry powder accumulation positioning firms for massive frontier tech bets.

stories.headline: Tulip and Preply Reach Unicorn Status with New Rounds
stories.summary: Factory monitoring platform Tulip hit $1.3B valuation after a $120M Series D, while language app Preply reached $1.2B via $150M Series D led by WestCap. Nearly 40 new 2026 unicorns are mostly AI-linked, reflecting continued investor appetite for category leaders.

stories.headline: Plaid Conducts Liquidity Round at $8 Billion Valuation
stories.summary: Fintech platform Plaid raised a liquidity round at $8B, down from prior peaks due to investor scrutiny on profitability. The round provides employee liquidity while reflecting broader fintech valuation tightening as VCs prioritize revenue resilience.

stories.headline: SparkLabs Launches $20M Fund with King Saud University
stories.summary: SparkLabs announced a $20M fund partnering with King Saud University to boost innovation in Saudi Arabia beyond Silicon Valley. The initiative addresses global VC geographic mismatches by blending academic talent with investor capital in emerging markets.

stories.headline: China's Solar Capacity Set to Surpass Coal for First Time in 2026
stories.summary: Rapid solar deployment driven by manufacturing ramp-ups and policy support will see clean energy reach nearly two-thirds of China's total power capacity by year-end. This shift contributes to flat or slightly declining carbon emissions as expanding clean power meets rising electricity demand.

stories.headline: Global Clean Energy Investment Hits Record $2.3 Trillion
stories.summary: An 8% rise from 2024 reflects surging commitments to solar, wind, and batteries projected to provide virtually all net new U.S. electricity capacity in 2026. Australia's main grid already draws 50% of demand from renewables with rooftop solar hitting 61% at peaks.

stories.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Push Oil Prices Into $90s Per Barrel
stories.summary: Chinese refiners suspended diesel and gasoline exports while Russia threatens European gas cuts amid Iran war disruptions described as the 'biggest disruption in oil production in history.' An LNG tanker rerouted from France to Asia signals intensifying Asian competition for supplies.

stories.headline: U.S. Faces Deepening Energy Affordability Crisis
stories.summary: Rising gas prices fueled by LNG exports combine with Trump policies forcing uneconomic coal plants to stay online, inflating costs despite cheaper solar-plus-battery alternatives. AI datacenter power demands and halted offshore wind projects via executive orders exacerbate supply challenges.

stories.headline: Tech Firms Build Private Power Plants for AI Data Centers
stories.summary: Companies are constructing on-site power plants to meet surging AI compute demands as public grid upgrades lag behind. The strategy increases carbon emissions and pollution but addresses critical power shortfalls threatening AI infrastructure expansion.

stories.headline: China Establishes National Low-Carbon Transition Fund
stories.summary: Announced during the Two Sessions, the fund targets growth in hydrogen power and green fuels alongside Electricity and Renewable Energy Law revisions. The initiative supports China's 0.3% drop in energy and industry emissions achieved in 2025.

stories.headline: Offshore Wind Proves Reliable During U.S. East Coast Winter Storm
stories.summary: Consistent power delivery from offshore wind projects during brutal cold snaps countered intermittency concerns and demonstrated grid reliability. The performance bolsters expansion cases despite recent U.S. policy halts on new offshore projects.

stories.headline: Novo Nordisk and Hims & Hers Resolve GLP-1 Legal Dispute via Partnership
stories.summary: Novo Nordisk and Hims & Hers ended their legal conflict over GLP-1 weight loss drugs by forming a partnership announced March 10. The settlement stems from prior disputes over compounded versions of these drugs, allowing both companies to collaborate in the booming GLP-1 market.

stories.headline: Biopharma Sector Shows Optimism Amid M&A Surge
stories.summary: A rise in mergers, acquisitions, and stock prices has lifted biotech from a multi-year slump entering 2026. However, high valuations, U.S. regulatory uncertainties at the FDA, and intensifying competition from Chinese drugmakers threaten sustained recovery.

stories.headline: BioMarin Presents at Major Healthcare Investor Conferences
stories.summary: BioMarin Pharmaceutical is presenting at four major investor events this month including TD Cowen, Leerink, and Jefferies Biotech Summit. The company's active conference schedule signals strong investor interest in its rare disease therapies pipeline.

stories.headline: ARPA-H Launches $144M PROSPR Program for Aging Biomarkers
stories.summary: The Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health is funding clinical trials to validate surrogate biomarkers of aging that don't require decades-long studies. Stanford-led researchers plan to develop a composite 'intrinsic capacity score' predicting death and disease over 20 years using health datasets and wearable data.

stories.headline: China's Solid-State EV Batteries Advance via Industry Collaboration
stories.summary: Major Chinese battery makers are jointly building facilities for solid-state battery materials production, aiming to cut costs and extend range. The collaboration responds to booming domestic EV demand and intensifying global competition in electrification and clean transport.

stories.headline: ARPA-H Commits $144M to First Large-Scale Aging Intervention Trials
stories.summary: The PROSPR program will identify and validate surrogate biomarkers enabling practical clinical trials of longevity interventions without decades-long studies. Stanford researchers plan a home test kit for personal aging assessment using wearable data and health datasets, representing a watershed moment for translating aging research.

stories.headline: Life Biosciences Wins FDA Approval for Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
stories.summary: Harvard-backed Life Biosciences received FDA approval for a Phase 1 trial using partial epigenetic reprogramming to restore vision in glaucoma patients. The approach uses three of four Yamanaka factors to rejuvenate cells while preserving function, representing the first human clinical application of cellular age-reversal.

stories.headline: Epigenetic Clock Velocity Emerges as Superior Mortality Predictor
stories.summary: Research from the InCHIANTI cohort shows the rate of change in epigenetic clocks predicts mortality risk better than static epigenetic age alone. This shifts longevity research focus from snapshot biomarker assessments to trajectory monitoring for earlier identification of accelerating aging.

stories.headline: GESDA Report: Longevity Science Crosses Clinical Translation Threshold
stories.summary: The 2026 GESDA Science Breakthrough Radar, informed by 2,390 researchers across 89 countries, identifies longevity science as having shifted from lifespan extension to healthspan optimization. A University of Zurich study showing Omega-3 fatty acids measurably slow biological aging is accelerating pharmaceutical development.

stories.headline: NASA Completes Artemis 2 Flight Readiness Review, Targets April Launch
stories.summary: All teams approved 'go' for the Artemis 2 crewed lunar flyby pending Vehicle Assembly Building work, targeting no earlier than April 1. The mission builds on prior uncrewed tests toward sustainable Moon exploration after overcoming SLS rocket and Orion spacecraft integration challenges.

stories.headline: SpaceX Launches Multiple Starlink Missions in Rapid Succession
stories.summary: SpaceX deployed 25 Starlink satellites from Vandenberg on March 13 followed by 29 more from Cape Canaveral on March 14, accelerating constellation growth. Reusable booster technology enables the high-frequency launch cadence meeting surging demand for global broadband coverage.

stories.headline: Isar Aerospace Spectrum Rocket Set for Second Test Flight
stories.summary: The European launch startup's 28-meter Spectrum rocket targets a March 19 test from Hawaii deploying 5 CubeSats to sun-synchronous orbit including ESA's Boost! experiment. The flight represents growing commercial launch competition as startups challenge SpaceX's dominance.

stories.headline: Roscosmos Launches Progress MS-33 ISS Resupply Mission
stories.summary: The Soyuz 2.1a cargo mission launched March 16-17 after months of delays caused by Baikonur launchpad damage in December 2025. The mission ensures crew sustainment and continuous human presence aboard the International Space Station.

stories.headline: China Conducts Multiple Satellite Launches Across Four Spaceports
stories.summary: CASC Long March rockets deployed Yaogan reconnaissance, SatNet telecom, and Tianqi IoT satellites from Taiyuan, Wenchang, Jiuquan, and Haiyang in early March. A Shijian 32 mission failed, highlighting reliability risks, while successful launches advanced China's surveillance and connectivity priorities.

stories.headline: Rocket Lab Deploys Open Cosmos Earth Observation Satellites
stories.summary: Rocket Lab's Electron launched the 'Cosmos Will See You Now' mission from New Zealand, orbiting Open Cosmos satellites for commercial Earth observation. The mission demonstrates Rocket Lab's frequent smallsat launch cadence supporting startup constellation growth.

stories.headline: March Madness 2026 Betting Projected to Hit Record $4 Billion
stories.summary: H2 Gambling Capital forecasts sportsbooks will process approximately $4 billion in wagers for the NCAA basketball tournament, surpassing prior records. Surging legal sports betting popularity, expanded online platforms, and the tournament's high-stakes drama fuel the growth.

stories.headline: Athletic Brands Launch Major Marketing Blitzes for Historic 2026 Sports Calendar
stories.summary: Nike and Adidas are ramping up campaigns amid a landmark year headlined by the FIFA World Cup in North America, with Nike relaunching ACG and Adidas opening a U.S.-only soccer flagship at American Dream Mall. The rare convergence of mega-events drives aggressive product launch strategies to dominate sneaker and apparel sales.

stories.headline: SBJ Names 2026's 10 Most Innovative Sports Tech Companies
stories.summary: Sports Business Journal honored firms including Bolt6, Owl AI, Recentive Analytics, and TMRW Sports for advancing officiating, performance analytics, and fan experiences. These companies have scaled to a third of NBA teams and NFL pilots leading to Super Bowl integrations.

stories.headline: U.S. Sports Media Landscape Shifts Amid Cord-Cutting Acceleration
stories.summary: SportBusiness Media's Market Watch report highlights cord-cutting and corporate restructurings as dominant trends reshaping U.S. sports media in 2026. Accelerating viewer shifts to streaming challenge traditional broadcasters while opening opportunities for digital platforms in rights deals.
